description: Moments provider currently mixes remote listener hydration with local Drift watch and uses upsert-only persistence, which can leave stale rows after remote deletes and split read source-of-truth.
resolution: Before moments PowerSync rollout, enforce one authoritative read path and add deletion reconciliation when applying remote snapshots to local storage.

name: Notification flows should use local-first reads
description: Unread count is realtime but notification list and moment deep-link navigation still depend on remote fetches; align notifications with local-first providers to support offline push-navigation and future PowerSync domain migration.

name: Moments single read SoT
description: Moments pipeline now uses local Drift as the single read source-of-truth while remote reconciliation is handled by a dedicated keepAlive sync controller started at app bootstrap (momentsSyncControllerProvider in moments_providers plus main.dart startup read).

title: Keep wrapper-based offline media rendering
rationale: PowerSync syncs relational metadata, not binary media bytes or signed URL validity. Moments and avatars should keep local-first wrapper widgets (OfflineImage/AvatarImage) and avoid repeating online/offline branching in feature screens.

name: PowerSync stream SQL join compatibility
description: PowerSync sync-config validation rejected moments stream queries using unsupported join patterns; replacing broad LEFT JOIN + OR visibility clauses with multiple simple JOIN/WHERE queries allowed Validate Sync Config to pass while preserving scope.

title: PowerSync validation gate split
rationale: Treat powersync validate as two gates: sync SQL/schema correctness and runtime connection availability. Passing Validate Sync Config confirms stream query compatibility; failing Test Connections with PS_DATABASE_URI missing indicates environment setup only and should not block code-level P1 status updates.

name: Notification moment navigation local-first
description: Notification navigation for moment details/map now resolves via PowerSync local SQLite first and falls back to repository only on local miss, reducing network dependency in push-entry flows.

name: BuildContext async gap hardening
description: Resolved all active use_build_context_synchronously diagnostics by adding mounted checks at each async boundary and avoiding stale dialog/sheet contexts after awaits in map, moments, and social screens.

title: PowerSync env naming convention
rationale: Use POWERSYNC_* env keys consistently: POWERSYNC_URL for client SDK endpoint and POWERSYNC_DATABASE_URI for service replication connection in powersync/service.yaml, replacing older PS_DATABASE_URI naming.

name: Chat mutation service naming
description: Renamed ChatOfflineService to ChatMutationService and updated all call sites to clarify responsibility: optimistic local chat writes plus PowerSync-managed upload queue, not a standalone sync engine.

name: Offline media policy service
description: Added OfflineMediaPolicyService to centralize media retention mode (smart/always/never), wifi-only behavior, cache limits, and per-moment/group pinning independent of PowerSync row sync.

name: Map camera target bridge in V2
description: MapPageV2 now listens to mapCameraTargetProvider so notification-driven map deep-links pan the camera; skipNextLocationUpdate is respected to avoid snapping back to user location after external targeting.

title: OfflineMediaPolicy left unwired for row-sync test
rationale: OfflineMediaPolicyService remains available but is not initialized in main startup so current validation isolates PowerSync row synchronization behavior without media policy side-effects.

name: MapV2 native clustering migration
description: MapPageV2 now uses Mapbox native GeoJsonSource clustering with CircleLayer/SymbolLayer for cluster rendering and tap-to-expansion via getGeoJsonClusterExpansionZoom; cluster visuals are no longer custom supercluster annotations.

name: Hybrid unclustered marker strategy
description: Unclustered map markers are rendered as PointAnnotations using custom bitmaps that prioritize the moment group cover image and overlay a user avatar badge, combining content-first and identity cues.
